raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 815 encourages local governments to increase curbside separation in their recycling programs to reduce contamination in the recycling stream.",
    "Many recycled materials become too dirty to be reused when thrown into the same bin.",
    "Several foreign countries have largely stopped importing U.S. recycled material because of contamination.",
    "AB 815 is necessary to improve the quality and marketability of recycled materials.",
    "Improving the quality and marketability of recycled materials will reduce the amount of these materials that are stockpiled, landfilled, or burned.",
    "Higher quality recyclables are more marketable for reuse and can be manufactured into new, higher-value products.",
    "The future of marketing recyclables in California will depend on the ability to reduce contamination and deliver cleaner material to processing plants."
  ]
}
```
